name: ALLIANCE Credit Union
description_short: Member-owned credit union headquartered in Lubbock, TX, offering checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and credit cards with a not-for-profit service model.
description_long:
ALLIANCE Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative based in Lubbock, Texas, with headquarters at 6219 Spur 327. The credit union operates multiple branches across the Lubbock area (Headquarters, Quaker Avenue, University, and 34th Street locations) and serves its members through a cooperative business model where all profits are reinvested into the organization.

The credit union offers comprehensive banking and lending services including personal checking and savings accounts (ALLIANCE Checking, Revive Checking, Liquid Asset, Money Market), certificates of deposit, IRAs, auto loans with refinancing options, student loans (including parent loans and refinancing), home mortgages (conventional, FHA, VA, USDA, construction, and self-employed programs), home equity lines of credit, Visa credit cards (standard and secured), commercial banking, and specialized products like their "Bits of Stock™" program. They also provide commercial checking and commercial loan options.

ALLIANCE distinguishes itself through its explicitly stated business model of offering the lowest rates on consumer loans while paying higher deposit rates than competitors, all while prioritizing member service quality over profitability. The credit union maintains 24/7 drive-through ATM access (ALLIANCE Express ITM) at multiple locations and employs dedicated mortgage loan officers and commercial loan officers. They also provide financial education resources, fraud prevention guidance, and active community engagement through the ACU Foundation.

As a traditional credit union with geographically limited membership (serving primarily Lubbock County, Texas), ALLIANCE is best suited for local members seeking competitive rates and personalized service rather than those requiring nationwide branch networks or digital-first banking experiences. The organization demonstrates transparency about scam prevention and security awareness, though specific APR ranges, membership eligibility requirements, and comparative rate data are not detailed on the public website.

As a financial institution, this bank competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders in the consumer lending space. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

tiers.name: Checking Account
features: No monthly maintenance fee (with qualifying deposit)

pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit structure ensures profits are reinvested for member benefit rather than shareholder returns
Explicitly advertises lowest consumer loan rates and higher deposit rates than competitors
24/7 ALLIANCE Express ITM drive-through ATM access at multiple locations for round-the-clock banking
Comprehensive mortgage offerings including FHA, VA, USDA, construction loans, and self-employed programs with dedicated loan officers
Multiple personal and business loan options including auto refinancing, student loan refinancing, and commercial lending
Active fraud prevention and security awareness with dedicated resources for identity theft and cybersecurity
Unique "Bits of Stock™" program offering fractional stock ownership tied to checking accounts
cons: Geographically limited to Lubbock, Texas area with only 4 branches, restricting access for non-local members
Website does not disclose specific APR ranges, rates, or membership eligibility requirements needed for rate comparison
No mention of online-only accounts, mobile banking capabilities, or digital-first services that modern consumers expect
Limited weekend hours (Saturday 8 a.m. to 1 p.m. only; closed Sundays) restrict in-person access for working members
No information about minimum balance requirements, account fees, or product-specific terms and conditions publicly available
best_for: Lubbock-area residents seeking competitive loan rates and personalized service from a member-owned institution
Homebuyers interested in specialized mortgage programs (FHA, VA, USDA, construction, self-employed loans) with dedicated loan officers
Small business owners in the Lubbock region needing commercial checking, loans, and renovation financing
Credit union members who prioritize not-for-profit banking ethics and reinvested profits over national bank convenience
diagnosis: ALLIANCE Credit Union is best for Lubbock-area residents and small business owners seeking a member-owned alternative to traditional banks with competitive rates and a not-for-profit mission. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this credit union only serves the Lubbock, Texas area and does not provide nationwide access or detail APR ranges online, requiring prospective members to contact them directly for rate and eligibility information.
services: Personal checking accounts (ALLIANCE Checking, Revive Checking)
Savings accounts (Liquid Asset, Money Market, Certificates of Deposit, IRAs, Junior Certificates)
Auto loans and auto loan refinancing with skip-a-pay option
Student loans and student loan refinancing (personal and parent loans)
Home mortgages (conventional, FHA, VA, USDA, construction, self-employed, H.O.M.E. 100 program)
Home equity lines of credit and second lien mortgages
Visa credit cards (standard Visa Advantage and secured Visa Secured cards)
Commercial checking and commercial loan options including renovation loans
24/7 ALLIANCE Express ITM drive-through ATM service
Fraud prevention, identity theft protection, and cybersecurity guidance
Bits of Stock™ fractional stock ownership program
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
